relationships.description: Looker API3 credentials: the client ID (jsonData.client_id) and client secret (secureJsonData.client_secret) are used together to obtain an access token via the Looker SDK's client-credentials login. Both are required when auth_type is 'client_secret' (pkg/models/config.go:33-40).

instructions.msg: This datasource has a single authentication method: 'client_secret' (Looker API3 credentials). jsonData.auth_type defaults to 'client_secret' and the config editor hides the auth-type selector because it is the only option (src/editors/configEditor.tsx:50, src/constants.ts:4-6). Configure jsonData.base_url, jsonData.client_id, and secureJsonData.client_secret.

Minimal payload: {"jsonData":{"base_url":"https://your-instance.looker.app","auth_type":"client_secret","client_id":"<your-client-id>"},"secureJsonData":{"client_secret":"<your-client-secret>"}}. All three of base_url, client_id, and client_secret are mandatory — the backend Validate() rejects an empty value with 'invalid/empty Looker base url', 'invalid/empty Looker client id', or 'invalid/empty Looker client secret' respectively (pkg/models/config.go:28-45). Create the client_id/client_secret pair as API3 credentials on a Looker user in the Looker admin UI.

Legacy/empty auth interpretation: there is no legacy storage shape. auth_type has only ever been 'client_secret', and the backend defaults a missing or empty auth_type to 'client_secret' in ApplyDefaults (pkg/models/config.go:47-50). Do not treat a missing jsonData.auth_type as unconfigured.

secureJsonData.client_secret is write-only: it can be set or reset but never read back. To check whether the secret is already configured on an existing datasource, read secureJsonFields.client_secret == true instead of secureJsonData.

jsonData.base_url is the Looker instance base URL, e.g. https://<instance>.looker.app. The backend trims surrounding whitespace and a single trailing slash (pkg/models/config.go:51-52); the Looker Go SDK then targets the '4.0' API version against that host (pkg/looker/client.go:22-28). Note: Grafana's standard datasource HTTP options (TLS skip-verify, timeout, proxy) are NOT applied to the Looker SDK client — the computed HTTPClientOptions are discarded (pkg/models/config.go:70-71) and looker.NewClient builds its own rtl.AuthSession (pkg/looker/client.go:22-38).
